title: Purifications: The Fallen Daimon
description: Empedocles on the divine soul exiled into mortal flesh and the cosmic cycles of Love and Strife.
introduction: Empedocles of Acragas was philosopher, physician, and mystic — a figure who claimed divine status and taught that the soul is a fallen daimon condemned to cycle through mortal incarnations until purified. His cosmology of four elements governed by Love (Philia) and Strife (Neikos) remained the standard model of physics until the scientific revolution.

translation.original_text: ὦ φίλοι, οἳ μέγα ἄστυ κατ᾿ ἀκρόπολιν Ἀκράγαντος ναίετ᾿ ἀν᾿ ἄκρα πόλεος, ἀγαθῶν μελεδήμονες ἔργων, χαίρετ᾿· ἐγὼ δ᾿ ὑμῖν θεὸς ἄμβροτος, οὐκέτι θνητός, πωλεῦμαι μετὰ πᾶσι τετιμένος.
translation.text: Friends, who dwell in the great city on the heights of Acragas, devoted to good works: I greet you. I go among you as an immortal god, no longer mortal, honored among all.

translation.original_text: ἔστιν Ἀνάγκης χρῆμα, θεῶν ψήφισμα παλαιόν, ἀΐδιον, πλατέεσσι κατεσφρηγισμένον ὅρκοις· εὖτέ τις ἀμπλακίῃσι φόνῳ φίλα γυῖα μιήνῃ... τρίς μιν μυρίας ὧρας ἀπὸ μακάρων ἀλάλησθαι.
translation.text: There is an oracle of Necessity, an ancient decree of the gods, eternal, sealed with broad oaths: whenever one of the divine spirits defiles his limbs with the sin of bloodshed... he must wander thrice ten thousand seasons away from the blessed ones.

translation.original_text: ἄλλοτε μὲν Φιλότητι συνερχόμεν᾿ εἰς ἓν ἅπαντα, ἄλλοτε δ᾿ αὖ δίχ᾿ ἕκαστα φορεύμενα Νείκεος ἔχθει.
translation.text: At one time all things come together into one through Love; at another, each is carried apart by the hatred of Strife.
